etymology.description: 不(不) (bù, "not") + 在(在) (zài, "in; at") + 酒(酒) (jiǔ, "wine; alcohol")
etymology.interpretation: The phrase 不在酒 (bù zài jiǔ, "not in the wine") suggests that the essence or true focus of something lies outside of the literal consumption of wine or alcohol. This can imply a deeper meaning or intent beyond what is immediately apparent.
